reason: Sample of 150 rows shows ~36 misclassifications (accuracy ~0.76), so even a perfect unseen remainder caps accuracy at ~0.80, well below the claimed 0.8897.
Per-row percentage errors average roughly 8-9% in the sample, about double the claimed 4.22% MAPE (under-reported lower-is-better metric).
Mean absolute error over the first ~40 sample rows is ~46, consistent with the claimed MAE of 44.04.
Values look uniform on [0,100] with a sample mean near 48-50, far below the claimed mean of 71.15.
Only 68 of 150 sample values are >=130 and the sample median sits near 118-122, suggesting the claimed 129.93 is inflated by ~6-9%, though uniform-median sampling noise leaves some doubt.

The visible 150 rows (a net-negative active stretch then a flat out-of-market regime) cover only 12% of 1250 days and cannot statistically refute the claimed cumulative return of an admittedly overfit backtest.
Many negatives score high (0.95, 0.86, 0.81, 0.77, 0.6+) and some positives score low (0.29, 0.42), implying hundreds of rank inversions and an AUC near 0.95-0.97, not 0.999.
The 150-row sample alone contains ~13 mismatches (accuracy ~0.91), while 0.999 on 569 rows would allow at most one error total.
Roughly 30 errors in 150 sample rows give ~0.80 accuracy with errors spread across classes, consistent with the claimed macro-F1 of 0.8052.
Mental average of per-row percentage errors over the first 20 sample rows is ~7.6%, matching the claimed 0.0767 MAPE.
